text.title: Feedback Collection, Categorization, and Processing Pipeline
text.summary: Feedback sources, categorization taxonomy, six-step processing pipeline, and agent workflow with prompt patterns.

list.item: Support tickets — high volume, high quality (real issues, concrete pain)
In-app feedback widget — medium volume, medium quality (prompted, context-aware)
App store reviews — medium volume, mixed quality (public, harder to follow up)
Sales calls — low volume, high quality (prospect pain, concrete context)
NPS surveys — medium volume, medium quality (prompted, sentiment-reliable)
User interviews — low volume, very high quality (deep intent, behavioral)
Social media — variable volume and quality (unsolicited, harder to categorize)

rule.statement: Lock the taxonomy in a versioned file. Treat taxonomy changes as schema migrations: write a migration script and re-tag existing items before applying.
rule.rationale: Taxonomy drift produces categorization inconsistency over time, making trend analysis unreliable. Versioning makes changes auditable and reversible.

list.item: Type: bug (something broken), request (new capability), enhancement (improve existing), confusion (UX/navigation issue), praise, complaint
Topic: create a product-specific taxonomy — typical: onboarding, core-feature-A, billing, integrations, performance, mobile, other

antipattern.reason: Enhancement becomes a black hole for uncategorized items. Force "bug" when verbatim contains "broken", "can't", "fails". Force specific topic or reject with request for taxonomy update.

section.title: Processing Pipeline

list.item: Step 1: Centralize — single source of truth (Canny, Productboard, Notion, or spreadsheet); auto-import from support + in-app
Step 2: Triage — type, topic, source, segment, sentiment per item; always preserve verbatim
Step 3: Aggregate — volume by category, trends over time, top requests by mention count
Step 4: Prioritize — connect to backlog items; weight by segment/ARR, not raw count
Step 5: Act — build (add to roadmap), won't do (document reason), need more info, already planned
Step 6: Close loop — email "you asked, we built" when feature ships; respond within 30 days of ship date

rule.statement: Weight feedback by ARR or segment, not raw mention count. One enterprise complaint can outweigh 50 free-tier votes.
rule.rationale: Volume bias: vocal free users post many tickets; at-risk enterprise accounts may post zero. Raw count ranking optimizes for the wrong population.

rule.statement: Always store the verbatim alongside structured fields. Never lose the original quote.
rule.rationale: Agent rewrites lose intent signals. Original wording reveals tone, context, and use case that structured fields cannot capture.

section.reference: Feedback table with category breakdown by month, top requests with segment and status.
Single-item triage: normalizes text, computes dedup hash, calls LLM with strict JSON schema, validates topic against taxonomy.

list.item: triage agent (per item): outputs structured JSON — type, topic, sentiment, segment, severity, dedup_hash, suggested_backlog_link
weekly aggregator (per week): groups by topic + dedup_id, surfaces top requests with mention counts and segment breakdown
close-loop agent (on deploy): watches deploy pipeline, drafts "you asked, we built" emails for human approval before send

rule.statement: Auto-responding without human review is a brand risk. Default mode = draft and queue; human approves before send.
rule.rationale: Wrong close-loop email (wrong feature attributed to wrong user, incorrect status) destroys trust harder than no email at all.
